title: carmichaelia australis
intro: Carmichaelia australis, also known as the scarlet pea or New Zealand bird's foot trefoil, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand. It is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, derived from its alkaloids and glycosides. The plant is believed to offer health benefits, such as aiding in digestion, reducing fever, and soothing respiratory issues. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties.
